target.name: Tekever
target.founder: Ricardo Mendes, Vitor Cristina, Pedro Sinogas

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $631M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Percepto
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Series C)

private.name: Monarch Tractor
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Series C)

data.narrative:
Tekever is a Series C-stage autonomous systems company in the Drones & Autonomous sector that has raised $631M to date. The comparable universe consists of six private companies operating in this space, including Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, Elroy Air, and Percepto. No public peers exist in the narrow same-stage, same-valuation-tier category.

A single valuation method proved applicable to this analysis. The Stage-Progression method uses the $631M total capital raised as a proxy baseline and applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C to subsequent-stage progression, yielding an implied valuation of $1.1B. A Peer-Median approach was not applicable due to insufficient comparable datapoints—while four same-sector peers exist, filtering by stage, timing, and valuation tier yielded fewer than three matches, making sector-median benchmarking unreliable.

The triangulation analysis supports a valuation range of $912M (low) to $1.2B (high), with a median point of $1.1B. This range reflects stage-appropriate valuation step-up assumptions applied to the company's funding history. Key uncertainties include the absence of tightly comparable peers at identical stage and scale, which limits cross-validation of the stage-progression result. Additional data on recent funding round terms, revenue run-rate, or loss metrics would strengthen conviction in the implied valuation band.
